info.title: mobu
info.description: Simulate user load for Science Platform testing

get.description: Return metadata about the running application. Can also be used as a health check. This route is not exposed outside the cluster and therefore cannot be used by external clients.
Metadata about the running version of mobu

schema.title: Response Get Flocks Mobu Flocks Get
Response Get Monkeys Mobu Flocks  Flock  Monkeys Get

get.description: Returns the monkey log output as a file

get.summary: Summary of statistics for a flock

post.description: Receives webhook events from the GitHub mobu CI app.
Receives webhook events from the GitHub mobu refresh app.

uidnumber.title: Numeric UID
uidnumber.description: If omitted, Gafaelfawr will assign a UID. (Gafaelfawr UID assignment requires Firestore be configured.)

gidnumber.title: Primary GID
gidnumber.description: If omitted but a UID was specified, use a GID equal to the UID. If both are omitted, Gafaelfawr will assign a UID and GID. (Gafaelfawr UID and GID assignment requires Firestore and synthetic user private groups to be configured.)

AuthenticatedUser.title: AuthenticatedUser
AuthenticatedUser.description: Represents an authenticated user with a token.

refreshing.title: If the business is currently in the process of refreshing

BusinessData.title: BusinessData
BusinessData.description:
Status of a running business.

Each type of business with additional data should create a new type
inheriting from this type and adding that information.

error_idle_time.title: How long to wait after an error before restarting

idle_time.title: How long to wait between business executions
idle_time.description: AFter each loop executing monkey business, the monkey will pause for this long

error_idle_time.title: How long to wait after an error before restarting

idle_time.title: How long to wait between business executions
idle_time.description: AFter each loop executing monkey business, the monkey will pause for this long

commit_url.title: GitHub URL to the commit being worked on

num_queued.title: Number of jobs waiting for a free worker

user.title: User that the worker works as

num_processed.title: Number of jobs this worker has processed since mobu started

current_job.title: The job the worker is currently running, if any

EmptyLoopConfig-Input.title: EmptyLoopConfig
EmptyLoopConfig-Input.description:
Configuration specialization for EmptyLoop.

This business class does nothing, successfully. It is used primarily for
testing mobu.

EmptyLoopConfig-Output.title: EmptyLoopConfig
EmptyLoopConfig-Output.description:
Configuration specialization for EmptyLoop.

This business class does nothing, successfully. It is used primarily for
testing mobu.

ErrorDetail.title: ErrorDetail
ErrorDetail.description: The detail of the error message.

users.title: Explicit list of users to run as
users.description: Run as the specific list of users. If specified, the length of the list must equal the count of monkeys to run. Specify either this or user_spec but not both.

user_spec.title: Specification to generate users
user_spec.description: Specify either this or users but not both

scopes.title: Token scopes
scopes.description: Must include all scopes required to run the business

FlockConfig-Input.title: FlockConfig
FlockConfig-Input.description:
Configuration for a flock of monkeys.

A flock must all share the same business and options, but may contain any
number of individual monkeys, which will all run as different users.

users.title: Explicit list of users to run as
users.description: Run as the specific list of users. If specified, the length of the list must equal the count of monkeys to run. Specify either this or user_spec but not both.

user_spec.title: Specification to generate users
user_spec.description: Specify either this or users but not both

scopes.title: Token scopes
scopes.description: Must include all scopes required to run the business

FlockConfig-Output.title: FlockConfig
FlockConfig-Output.description:
Configuration for a flock of monkeys.

A flock must all share the same business and options, but may contain any
number of individual monkeys, which will all run as different users.

business.title: Name of the business the flock is running

start_time.title: When the flock was started
start_time.description: Will be null if the flock hasn't started

monkey_count.title: Number of monkeys in the flock

success_count.title: Total number of monkey successes in flock

failure_count.title: Total number of monkey failures in flock

FlockSummary.title: FlockSummary
FlockSummary.description: Summary statistics about a running flock.

error_idle_time.title: How long to wait after an error before restarting

idle_time.title: How long to wait between business executions
idle_time.description: AFter each loop executing monkey business, the monkey will pause for this long

lfs_read_url.title: LFS read URL for Git-LFS enabled repo

lfs_write_url.title: LFS write URL for Git-LFS enabled repo

GitLFSBusinessOptions-Input.description: Options for business that runs git LFS operations.

Index.title: Index
Index.description:
Metadata returned by the external root URL of the application.

Notes
-----
As written, this is not very useful. Add additional metadata that will be
helpful for a user exploring the application, or replace this model with
some other model that makes more sense to return from the application API
root.

user.title: User as which the monkey is running

refreshing.title: If the business is currently in the process of refreshing

image.title: Jupyter lab image information
image.description: Will only be present when there is an active Jupyter lab

notebook.title: Name of the currently running notebook
notebook.description: Will not be present if no notebook is being executed

running_code.title: Currently running code
running_code.description: Will not be present if no code is being executed

NotebookRunnerData.title: NotebookRunnerData
NotebookRunnerData.description: Status of a running NotebookRunner business.

error_idle_time.title: How long to wait after an error before restarting

idle_time.title: How long to wait between business executions
idle_time.description: AFter each loop executing monkey business, the monkey will pause for this long

delete_lab.title: Whether to delete the lab between iterations
delete_lab.description: By default, the lab is deleted and recreated after each iteration of monkey business. Set this to false to keep the same lab.

execution_idle_time.title: How long to wait between cell executions

get_node.title: Whether to get the node name for error reporting
get_node.description: Used by NubladoPythonLoop and its subclasses. Requires the lab have lsst.rsp pre-installed.

jitter.title: Maximum random time to pause
jitter.description: If set to a non-zero value, pause for a random interval between 0 and that interval before logging in to JupyterHub, and between each iteration of the core execution loop. Use this when running lots of monkeys for load testing to spread their execution sequence out more realistically and avoid a thundering herd problem.

jupyter_timeout.title: HTTP client timeout for Jupyter requests
jupyter_timeout.description: Used as the connect, read, and write timeout for talking to either JupyterHub or Jupyter lab.

max_websocket_message_size.title: Maximum length of WebSocket message (in bytes)
max_websocket_message_size.description: This has to be large enough to hold HTML and image output from executing notebook cells, even though we discard that data. Set to `null` for no limit.

spawn_settle_time.title: How long to wait before polling spawn progress
spawn_settle_time.description: Wait this long after triggering a lab spawn before starting to poll its progress. KubeSpawner 1.1.0 has a bug where progress queries prior to starting the spawn will fail with an exception that closes the progress EventStream.

max_executions.title: How much to execute in a given lab and session
max_executions.description: NotebookRunner goes through the directory of notebooks one-by-one, running the entirety of each one and starting again at the beginning of the list when it runs out, until it has executed a total of `max_executions` notebooks. It then closes the session (and optionally deletes and recreates the lab, controlled by `delete_lab`), and then picks up where it left off.

repo_branch.title: Git branch of notebook repository to execute

repo_ref.title: Git ref of notebook repository to execute

repo_url.title: Git URL of notebook repository to execute

notebooks_to_run.title: Specific notebooks to run
notebooks_to_run.description: If this is set, then only these specific notebooks will be executed.

exclude_dirs.title: Any notebooks in these directories will not be run
exclude_dirs.description: These directories are relative to the repo root. Any notebooks in child directories of these directories will also be excluded. Only used by the NotebookRunner.

error_idle_time.title: How long to wait after an error before restarting

idle_time.title: How long to wait between business executions
idle_time.description: AFter each loop executing monkey business, the monkey will pause for this long

delete_lab.description: By default, the lab is deleted and recreated after each iteration of monkey business. Set this to false to keep the same lab.

get_node.description: Used by NubladoPythonLoop and its subclasses. Requires the lab have lsst.rsp pre-installed.

Use this when running lots of monkeys for load testing to spread their execution sequence out more realistically and avoid a thundering herd problem.

It then closes the session (and optionally deletes and recreates the lab, controlled by `delete_lab`), and then picks up where it left off.

SolitaryConfig.title: SolitaryConfig
SolitaryConfig.description:
Configuration for a solitary monkey.

This is similar to `~mobu.models.flock.FlockConfig`, but less complex
since it can only wrap a single monkey business.

SolitaryResult.title: SolitaryResult
SolitaryResult.description: Results from executing a solitary monkey.

stop.title: End of event
stop.description: Will be null if the event is ongoing

elapsed.title: Duration of event
elapsed.description: Will be null if the event is ongoing

running_query.title: Currently running query
running_query.description: Will not be present if no query is being executed

uidnumber.title: Numeric UID
uidnumber.description: If omitted, Gafaelfawr will assign a UID. (Gafaelfawr UID assignment requires Firestore be configured.)

gidnumber.title: Primary GID
gidnumber.description: If omitted but a UID was specified, use a GID equal to the UID. If both are omitted, Gafaelfawr will assign a UID and GID. (Gafaelfawr UID and GID assignment requires Firestore and synthetic user private groups to be configured.)

User.title: User
User.description: Configuration for the user whose credentials the monkey will use.

username_prefix.title: Prefix for usernames
username_prefix.description: Each user will be formed by appending a number to this

uid_start.title: Starting UID
uid_start.description: Users will be given consecutive UIDs starting with this. If omitted, Gafaelfawr will assign UIDs. (Gafaelfawr UID assignment requires Firestore be configured.)

gid_start.title: Starting GID
gid_start.description: Users will be given consecutive primary GIDs starting with this. If omitted but UIDs were given, the GIDs will be equal to the UIDs. If both are omitted, Gafaelfawr will assign UIDs and GIDs (which requires Firestore and synthetic user private groups to be configured).

UserSpec.title: UserSpec
UserSpec.description: Configuration to generate a set of users.
